posts.text: You don’t have to even be like ‘it’s capitalism, silly’ bc obviously that’s just a very unsatisfying and annoying answer. But we are all robbed of community right now and every time we turn around we are getting robbed and shoved back into our car. Of course everyone’s mad.
posts.quote: You don’t have to even be like ‘it’s capitalism, silly’ bc obviously that’s just a very unsatisfying and annoying answer. But we are all robbed of community right now and every time we turn around we are getting robbed and shoved back into our car. Of course everyone’s mad.

posts.text:
Everything is downstream from the deep alienation people feel these days from loss of community. Immigrants aren’t why your kids don’t play in the park together or ride their bikes to some sandlot. That’s roads, helicopter parenting, relentless competition/activities .


M. Nolan Gray 🥑 (@mnolangray)

I often see conservative content like this, and I wonder: do they not realize stuff like this still happens? Every city and suburb in the country hosts a procession of family-focused summer events.

posts.text: but it’s aligned more w a class bound/coastal college graduate morality that’s very lightly felt but passionately argued. You can get mad at me and hop around and say ‘BUT WHAT ABOUT THE KIDS’ but really the questions should just be about making the books fair which is boring.

posts.text: Feels very c Dolores Tucker right now especially since the anti-gambling consensus among the media is so robust. There needs to be regulation all the way up to banning a lot of prop bets, etc but this kinda feels like one of those things where there’s a lot of bluster…

posts.text:
I think this is well written but the gap between the media and normies on this issue is extremely wide. People just want less ads they don’t think sports betting in general is some horrific vice that will destroy young men.


McKay Coppins (@mckaycoppins)

Last year, The Atlantic gave me $10K to gamble with. What started as a journalistic gimmick turned into something more... unnerving. 

My cover story on the online betting boom warping sports, culture, politics, and the psyches of millions of
